selection_reason: Top-3 regressed benchmarks by (latest - reference)/reference drift, from benchmarks/data/reference.json vs benchmarks/data/latest.json. user_priority.md names matmul/spectral_norm as future tier2_float_loop targets but those have drift 2.2% / -3.2% (not in top 3 regressors) so they are not included in this round's CONTEXT_GATHER. Selection is drift-driven per CONTEXT_GATHER prompt.

candidates.hot_function: vec3_add (inner) and create_and_sum (outer loop); new_vec3 is the allocator

disasm_paths: /tmp/cg_object_creation_disasm.txt (llvm-objdump unavailable for raw binary; structured counts below come from a Python ARM64 top-level decoder over /tmp/gscript_object_creation_t2.bin)

ir_summary_create_and_sum.note: Inner loop B1 has 2 Call ops per iteration: Call v1 (new_vec3 via globals[0]) and Call v19 (vec3_add via globals[5]). Neither callee is inlined. v29 Phi is typed :any (vec3 object stays boxed).

ir_summary_vec3_add.note: GetField v3..v10 all typed :any — no GuardType float, no unboxing. Add ops are untyped (v5=Add:any, v8=Add:any, v11=Add:any) — generic dispatch, not AddFloat.

disasm_summary_create_and_sum.classifier_note: Top-level ARM64 decoder over raw instruction words; BL/BLR counted as call; B/B.cond/CBZ/CBNZ/TBZ/TBNZ/BR/RET counted as branch.

observations.text: vec3_add is NOT inlined. Each object_creation inner-loop iteration makes 2 native-style Calls (to new_vec3 and vec3_add) across a Tier2→Tier2 boundary. create_and_sum IR shows 4 Call ops total, transform_chain shows 5. See /tmp/cg_TestContextGather_ObjectCreation.log lines 11, 21, 23.
observations.rationale: directly visible in logged IR; Call ops enumerated

observations.text: vec3_add has 6 GetField ops, all with result type :any (NOT typed float). There is no GuardType chain attaching float to these loads. /tmp/cg_vec3.log lines showing 'v3 = GetField v0.field[1] : any' through 'v10 = GetField v1.field[3] : any'.
observations.rationale: directly visible; IR printer shows : any tag per value

observations.text: vec3_add's 3 Add ops are generic Add (v5/v8/v11 = Add … : any), not AddFloat. This means the inner hot path in object_creation dispatches through the generic any+any handler, not the float fast path — even though all inputs are floats at runtime.

Memory-heavy instruction mix: create_and_sum disasm is 466/813 (57%) memory, only 236/813 (29%) compute. 12 spill stores + 12 spill loads to [sp, #imm] are present even though the IR reports NumSpills=0 — the spills are in the Call prolog/epilog save area, not register-allocator spills.
observations.rationale: instruction counts are from a top-level ARM64 decoder (not symbolic disasm); the 12 str/ldr-sp counts are exact but attribution to prolog vs spill is inferred

observations.text: new_vec3 IR is 1 block, 3 SetFields + 1 NewTable + Return. Allocation path is lean; the bottleneck is NOT in the allocator's IR shape — any object_creation regression is more likely in (a) the NewTable runtime cost, (b) the Call overhead to reach new_vec3, or (c) the unboxed-float miss in vec3_add.
observations.rationale: IR structure is directly visible; ranking of causes is inferred

ir_summary.note: lo/hi are LoadSlot :any then GuardType int (v59, v60). Loop body uses GetTable/SetTable on v0 (arr). 2 Call ops in B6 are recursive quicksort Calls on exit.

observations.text: quicksort has 2 Call ops (the self-recursive quicksort(arr, lo, i-1) and quicksort(arr, i+1, hi)). Given the reference→latest regression of +19%, and the 598bc1e commit 'fix: self-call DirectEntryPtr check prevents goroutine stack overflow' which added a runtime check on the recursive self-call path, this commit is a high-prior bisect candidate for sort.
observations.rationale: Call-op count and commit message are both directly visible; causal attribution is inferred and must be confirmed by git bisect

observations.text: No float/SIMD instructions in sort disasm (0 fp_simd_insns). Workload is integer + pointer only. Regression cannot be attributed to any float-path change.

Instruction mix is compute-heavy (635/1602 = 40%) vs memory (682/1602 = 43%). Only 6 spill str/ldr pairs — not spill-bound. The 283 branch instructions (18%) are the loop control + partition comparisons. No obvious code-bloat signal.

IR shows GuardType int on lo/hi (v59, v60) but the AddInt/SubInt/LeInt ops in the partition loop use the guarded ints directly — int specialization IS working for the inner partition loop. The regression is NOT a loss of int-type specialization.
observations.rationale: directly visible in logged IR (v3 = LeInt v59 v60, v11 = SubInt v59 v10, etc.)

ir_summary.note: Loop body B1: GetGlobal coroutine, GetField field[2] (resume), Call resume(co). Also Add v24+v13 : any even though v24 is Phi :int — generic add emitted.

observations.text: Tier 2 IR for test_yield_loop is tiny (4 blocks, 561 ARM64 insns). The hot cost of coroutine_bench is NOT in compiled Tier 2 code; it is in the native-call path into coroutine.resume / coroutine.yield / coroutine.create (goroutine switch). Tier 2 optimization cannot meaningfully move this benchmark — runtime-side improvements (e.g. pre-grown goroutine stack, faster native transition) are required.
observations.rationale: IR block count and call counts are directly visible; attribution to native path is based on the absence of significant IR work and the well-known coroutine runtime cost

observations.text: test_yield_loop emits 'v15 = Add v24, v13 : any' where v24 is a Phi typed :int. The loop accumulator is int but Add falls back to the generic any path. This is a missed type-specialization opportunity but its impact on a coroutine-bound benchmark is LOW.
observations.rationale: IR tag is directly visible; impact estimate is inferred

observations.text: Bisect-candidate-wise: 598bc1e (self-call DirectEntryPtr check) and 39b5ef3 (Shape system, GC scan all regs, split table.go, empty-loop fix) are the two large code-affecting commits between the reference baseline (772bfa7) and HEAD (3ce3dd7). Neither obviously targets coroutine.resume, but a GC-scan-all-regs change could increase goroutine-switch cost if more registers are now root-scanned per switch.
observations.rationale: speculation from commit messages; requires bisect to confirm

observations.text: test_yield_loop has only 2 GetField ops (coroutine.resume lookup) and 2 Call ops per iteration. Memory / compute / branch ratio is 298/196/58 — not spill-bound (6 str-sp / 6 ldr-sp).

bisect_candidates.subject: fix: self-call DirectEntryPtr check prevents goroutine stack overflow
bisect_candidates.why_suspect: adds a runtime check on the recursive self-call fast path — quicksort is recursive (sort +19%), test_yield_loop's body invokes coroutine.resume which re-enters via goroutine, transform_chain/create_and_sum call vec3_add repeatedly. All three top regressors go through call-heavy paths.

bisect_candidates.subject: fix: Shape system, GC scan all regs, split table.go, fix empty-loop test
bisect_candidates.why_suspect: Shape system change alters table field access paths; GC-scan-all-regs can lengthen goroutine scan cost (possible coroutine_bench link); table.go split touches NewTable/SetField codegen used by object_creation's new_vec3.

bisect_candidates.subject: opt: R28 Task 1 — ctx.Regs lazy flush on self-call fast path
bisect_candidates.why_suspect: touches the self-call fast path (sort recursion)

bisect_candidates.subject: opt: R32 Task 1 — LoopScalarPromotionPass for nbody float loop-carried pairs
bisect_candidates.why_suspect: R32 landed a new Tier 2 pass. If the gate matches on unintended shapes, it could affect vec3_add / create_and_sum path. Low prior: R32 post-mortem says the gate was too strict, not too loose.

bisect_candidates.subject: opt: R31 Task 1 — SimplifyPhisPass (Braun Algorithm 5)
bisect_candidates.why_suspect: another new Tier 2 pass touching phi rewriting; object_creation/sort/coroutine all have phis in their inner loops.

forbidden_patterns_avoided: No hand-constructed Function fixtures were used
No TypeFloat/TypeInt nodes were manually synthesized
All IR came from profileTier2Func -> RunTier2Pipeline(fn, nil) on real benchmarks/suite/*.gs source
tooling_caveats: llvm-objdump (Xcode 15 ships version that does not support -b binary) and otool (requires Mach-O not raw bytes) could not symbolically disassemble the raw JIT code bytes. A Python ARM64 top-level-encoding decoder was used instead to classify instructions by major category (compute/memory/branch/call/fp_simd) and to count STR/LDR [SP,#imm] spill-region accesses. This is exact for category counts but does not give mnemonic-level detail. ANALYZE should run a focused disasm (e.g. by embedding the bytes in a minimal object file or using the project's own diagnostic tooling) if instruction-level sequence is needed.
